message:
feat(runtime): injectable time and sleep hooks for BlockingPool

Make the blocking thread pool's timeout accounting fully deterministic
by introducing two configurable hooks in BlockingPoolOptions:

- time_getter: fn() -> Instant — replaces all Instant::now() calls in
  timeout deadline computation, idle retirement checks, and completion
  wait loops. Defaults to wall_clock_now (Instant::now).
- sleep_fn: fn(Duration) — replaces thread::sleep in the shutdown wait
  loop, allowing tests to advance a synthetic clock without blocking
  the host thread. Defaults to blocking_thread_sleep (thread::sleep).

Internal changes:
- BlockingTaskCompletion now takes a TimeGetter for its wait_timeout
  condvar loop, using timeout_deadline/timeout_remaining helpers.
- blocking_worker_loop uses inner.time_getter for idle-since tracking.
- shutdown_and_wait uses both hooks for its polling loop.
- BlockingPoolOptions Debug impl shows whether custom hooks are set
  using std::ptr::fn_addr_eq.
- builder.rs: Use ..Default::default() to pick up new fields.

New deterministic tests:
- completion_wait_timeout_uses_custom_time_getter: Verifies that a
  stepped time function drives wait_timeout without wall sleep.
- shutdown_and_wait_uses_custom_time_and_sleep_hooks: Verifies that
  an advancing clock + sleep hook drive shutdown timeout accounting,
  confirming the sleep hook is called and advances the synthetic clock
  through the full timeout budget.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
